framework_freeze.reason: All freeze criteria met: 5 real projects with no false-project pattern, zero provenance violations, every prior recurring question now auto-resolved by a codified rule, genuinely-unresolved review rate 2.0% (<10%), and empty cases handled with no forced candidates. Isolated exceptions (Kibur RAG identity, career-mentor merge, webview ownership) are case-specific and do not block freeze.

ready_for_another_pilot.reason: No systemic failure recurred across >=3 cases. Remaining items are isolated case-specific exceptions, which by policy must not trigger another pilot.

ready_for_50_100_case_subscription_batch.reason: Recurring rules generalized across the 40 blind cases with low residual human review; the frozen rule set is ready to scale to 50-100 cases.

ready_for_full_corpus_subscription_extra.reason: Ready for staged scale-up under the frozen schema, BUT two upstream data issues persist from 4D and should be monitored: (1) some 'user_authored' segments still embed pasted assistant/tool content (mixed-origin segmentation), and (2) attachment filenames remain largely null in 3R-P so cross-conversation attachment identity relies on hashes. Neither blocks correctness here (handled by hr-018), but both add review load at full scale.

ready_for_api_automation.reason: Now that the schema and 22 rules are frozen, automation can inherit fixed conventions. Gate behind one 50-100 case subscription batch confirming the genuinely-unresolved rate stays <10% at larger N before enabling batch-API automation.
